name: create_file
description: Create a new file with specified content
systemPrompt: Use this skill to create new files. Always use absolute paths starting with the user's working directory. Organize project files in descriptive subdirectories (e.g., web_app/, api_server/). Never overwrite existing files without user confirmation - use read_file first to check if a file exists.

name: read_file
description: Read contents of a file with optional chunking for large files
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read file contents. For large files exceeding context limits, the skill will return file info and suggest using chunkIndex to read in parts. Use startLine/endLine for specific ranges, or chunkIndex (0-based) to read sequential chunks. Default chunkSize is 500 lines. When a file returns warning='FILE_TOO_LARGE', iterate through chunks with chunkIndex=0, 1, 2... to process the entire file.

name: update_file
description: Update an existing file with new content
systemPrompt: Use this skill to modify existing files. IMPORTANT: Always read the file first with read_file to understand current content before updating. When fixing bugs or making improvements, use this skill directly rather than just showing corrected code. Include the complete new file content, not just the changes.

name: delete_file
description: Delete a file from the filesystem
systemPrompt: Use this skill to delete files. For fuzzy requests like 'delete the old config' or 'remove that temp file', ALWAYS use list_directory first to see what files exist, then match to the user's intent before deleting. Never delete files without being confident about which file the user wants removed.

name: delete_directory
description: Delete a directory and all its contents recursively
systemPrompt: Use this skill to delete directories. CRITICAL: For fuzzy requests like 'delete the security folder' or 'remove that project directory', ALWAYS use list_directory FIRST to see what directories exist, then match to the user's intent before deleting. This is a destructive operation - be certain about the target.

name: list_directory
description: List all files and directories in a path
systemPrompt: Use this skill FIRST when the user gives a fuzzy or imprecise request about files or directories (e.g., 'delete the security folder', 'find the config file', 'what files are here'). This is the discovery skill - use it to understand what exists before taking action. Always list before delete/move operations when the exact path is unclear.

name: move_file
description: Move or rename a file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to move or rename files. For fuzzy requests like 'rename that file' or 'move the config somewhere else', use list_directory first to identify the exact source file. Ensure the destination directory exists or will be created.

name: copy_file
description: Copy a file to a new location
systemPrompt: Use this skill to copy files while preserving the original. Useful for creating backups before modifications or duplicating templates. For fuzzy requests, use list_directory first to identify the exact source file.

name: fetch_url
description: Fetch content from a URL
systemPrompt: Use this skill for simple HTTP requests. For JavaScript-rendered pages or sites with bot detection, prefer playwright_fetch instead. Good for APIs, RSS feeds, and simple HTML pages. Set appropriate timeout for slow endpoints.

name: dns_lookup
description: Perform DNS lookup for a domain
systemPrompt: Use this skill to resolve domain names to IP addresses. Useful for network diagnostics, verifying DNS configuration, or checking if a domain resolves correctly.

name: check_port
description: Check if a port is open on a host
systemPrompt: Use this skill to test network connectivity to specific ports. Useful for verifying services are running, checking firewall rules, or diagnosing connection issues. Keep timeout short (2-5 seconds) for responsiveness.

name: ping_host
description: Ping a host to check connectivity
systemPrompt: Use this skill to test basic network connectivity to a host. Useful for diagnosing network issues or verifying a server is reachable. Default count is 4 pings; use fewer for quick checks.

name: http_request
description: Make custom HTTP requests (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
systemPrompt: Use this skill for API calls that need custom methods, headers, or request bodies. For simple GETs, fetch_url may be simpler. Always include Content-Type header when sending JSON body. For JS-rendered pages, use playwright_fetch.

name: run_bash
description: Execute bash commands on Linux/macOS
systemPrompt: Use this skill sparingly for shell commands that cannot be accomplished with other skills. Disabled by default for security. When enabled, avoid commands that could damage the system. Prefer specific skills (git_status, list_processes, etc.) over raw shell commands.

name: run_python
description: Execute Python code
systemPrompt: Use this skill to run Python scripts when no specific skill exists for the task. Disabled by default for security. When enabled, ensure code is safe and doesn't access sensitive data or perform destructive operations.

name: system_info
description: Get system information (CPU, memory, disk)
systemPrompt: Use this skill when the user asks about system resources, hardware specs, or needs to diagnose performance issues. Provides CPU cores/usage, memory usage, and disk space. Good first step for system diagnostics.

name: parse_json
description: Parse and validate JSON data
systemPrompt: Use this skill to parse and validate JSON strings. Useful for verifying JSON syntax, extracting data from JSON responses, or debugging malformed JSON. Returns the parsed data structure and its type.

name: parse_csv
description: Parse CSV data into structured format
systemPrompt: Use this skill to parse CSV data into a list of dictionaries. Assumes first row contains headers. Specify delimiter for non-comma separators (e.g., tab, semicolon). Returns row count and column names.

name: base64_encode
description: Encode data to Base64
systemPrompt: Use this skill to encode text data to Base64 format. Useful for encoding data for URLs, APIs, or embedding binary data in text formats. For decoding, use base64_decode.

name: base64_decode
description: Decode Base64 string(s). Pass a single base64 string OR a block of text containing one or more base64 strings — the skill auto-detects and decodes each one.
systemPrompt: ALWAYS call this tool when the user asks you to decode, read, or reveal Base64 content. Do NOT attempt Base64 decoding in your head — you will get it wrong. Pass the entire relevant text as 'text'; the skill will find every Base64-looking substring, attempt decoding, and return the successfully decoded results. Works for a single isolated string OR a larger blob with multiple embedded Base64 payloads.

name: hash_data
description: Generate hash (MD5, SHA1, SHA256) of data
systemPrompt: Use this skill to generate cryptographic hashes of data. Supports MD5 (fast, not secure), SHA1 (legacy), SHA256 (recommended), and SHA512. Useful for checksums, data integrity verification, or generating unique identifiers.

name: generate_uuid
description: Generate a UUID (v4)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to generate unique identifiers. UUID v4 is random and suitable for most use cases like database IDs, session tokens, or unique file names.

name: get_timestamp
description: Get current timestamp in various formats
systemPrompt: Use this skill to get the current date/time. Supports formats: 'iso' (ISO 8601), 'unix' (epoch seconds), 'date' (YYYY-MM-DD), 'time' (HH:MM:SS), 'datetime' (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS), or custom strftime format.

name: find_patterns
description: Search for regex patterns in text
systemPrompt: Use this skill to search for patterns in text using regular expressions. Supports flags: 'i' (case-insensitive), 'm' (multiline), 's' (dotall). Returns all matches with positions. Useful for extracting emails, URLs, phone numbers, or custom patterns.

name: analyze_code
description: Analyze code for basic metrics
systemPrompt: Use this skill to get basic code metrics: line counts, comment density, average line length. Useful for quick code quality assessment. For deeper analysis, read the code directly and apply your knowledge.
code:
def execute(params):
    """Analyze code for basic metrics."""
    code = params.get('code')
    if not code:
        return {'success': False, 'error': 'code parameter is required'}

    language = params.get('language', 'unknown')
    lines = code.split('\n')
    non_empty_lines = [l for l in lines if l.strip()]

    # Detect comment patterns based on language
    comment_patterns = ['#', '//', '/*', '*', '<!--']
    comment_lines = [l for l in lines if any(l.strip().startswith(p) for p in comment_patterns)]

    return {
        'success': True,
        'language': language,
        'total_lines': len(lines),
        'code_lines': len(non_empty_lines),
        'comment_lines': len(comment_lines),
        'blank_lines': len(lines) - len(non_empty_lines),
        'avg_line_length': sum(len(l) for l in lines) / len(lines) if lines else 0
    }

name: count_words
description: Count words in text
systemPrompt: Use this skill to count words, unique words, characters, and lines in text. Useful for content analysis, document statistics, or verifying content length requirements.

name: compress_data
description: Compress data using gzip
systemPrompt: Use this skill to compress text data using gzip. Returns Base64-encoded compressed data. Useful for reducing data size for storage or transmission. Use decompress_data to restore.

name: decompress_data
description: Decompress gzip data
systemPrompt: Use this skill to decompress gzip-compressed data that was encoded with compress_data. Input should be Base64-encoded gzip data.

name: run_powershell
description: Execute PowerShell commands on Windows
systemPrompt: Use this skill for Windows-specific tasks that require PowerShell. Disabled by default for security. Only available on Windows systems. Prefer specific skills over raw PowerShell commands when possible.

name: run_cmd
description: Execute cmd.exe commands on Windows
systemPrompt: Use this skill for Windows cmd.exe commands. Disabled by default for security. For PowerShell-specific features, use run_powershell instead. Only available on Windows systems.

name: get_windows_services
description: List Windows services and their status
systemPrompt: Use this skill to list Windows services or check a specific service status. Disabled by default. Only available on Windows. Useful for system administration and troubleshooting.

name: get_registry_value
description: Read Windows registry value
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read Windows registry values. Disabled by default for security. Only available on Windows. Useful for checking system configuration or application settings.

name: set_registry_value
description: Write Windows registry value (requires admin)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to write Windows registry values. Disabled by default - DANGEROUS. Requires administrator privileges. Only use when explicitly requested and you understand the consequences.

name: ocr_image
description: Extract text from an image using OCR (requires pytesseract)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to extract text from images (screenshots, scanned documents, photos of text). Requires pytesseract and Tesseract OCR installed. Specify language code for non-English text (e.g., 'deu' for German, 'fra' for French).

name: unzip_file
description: Extract a ZIP archive to a directory
systemPrompt: Use this skill to extract ZIP archives. If extractPath is not specified, extracts to the same directory as the ZIP file. Returns list of extracted files.

name: zip_files
description: Create a ZIP archive from files/directories
systemPrompt: Use this skill to create ZIP archives from files or directories. Supports multiple source paths. Directories are added recursively. Uses deflate compression.

name: read_email_file
description: Read and parse email files (.eml/.msg) with attachment content extraction, nested emails, and QR code URL detection
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read saved email files. Extracts subject, from, to, cc, date, body, and attachments. Enhanced features: extracts text content from PDF/DOCX/XLSX attachments, parses nested emails (.eml within .msg including embedded message objects), and detects URLs in QR codes from image attachments. Set extractAttachments=false to skip attachment content extraction for faster processing.

name: get_file_metadata
description: Get file metadata (size, dates, permissions)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to get detailed file information: size, creation/modification/access dates, permissions, and whether it's a file or directory. Useful for file management and troubleshooting.

name: search_files
description: Search for files by name pattern in a directory
systemPrompt: Use this skill to find files matching a pattern (e.g., '*.py', 'config*', '*.log'). Supports glob patterns. Set recursive=true to search subdirectories. Returns full paths of matching files.

name: download_file
description: Download a file from a URL
systemPrompt: Use this skill to download files from URLs. If savePath is not specified, saves to current directory with filename from URL. For web pages, use fetch_url or playwright_fetch instead.

name: screenshot
description: Take a screenshot and save to file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to capture a screenshot of the primary monitor. Requires the mss package. Saves as PNG. Useful for documentation, bug reports, or visual verification.

name: convert_image
description: Convert image format (PNG, JPG, BMP, GIF, etc.)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to convert images between formats (PNG, JPG, BMP, GIF, TIFF, etc.). Requires Pillow. If format is not specified, infers from output file extension.

name: list_processes
description: List running processes with PID, name, CPU%, and memory usage (cross-platform)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to see running processes. Sort by 'cpu', 'memory', 'name', or 'pid'. Limit results for readability. Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux). Useful for system monitoring and finding resource-heavy processes.

name: kill_process
description: Kill a process by PID or name (cross-platform)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to terminate processes. Can kill by PID (specific) or name (all matching). DANGEROUS: Disabled by default. Use list_processes first to identify the correct process. Cross-platform.

name: start_process
description: Start a process in the background (cross-platform)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to launch background processes. DANGEROUS: Disabled by default. Process runs detached from the terminal. Returns the PID of the started process. Cross-platform.

name: create_directory
description: Create a new directory (creates parent directories if needed)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to create directories. Automatically creates parent directories if they don't exist (like mkdir -p). Use absolute paths starting with the user's working directory.

name: playwright_fetch
description: Fetch webpage content using Playwright browser with stealth mode (bypasses bot detection, handles JS-rendered pages)
systemPrompt: Use this skill for web pages that require JavaScript rendering or have bot detection. Better than fetch_url for modern websites, SPAs, and protected content. Supports multiple URLs in parallel. Falls back to simple HTTP if Playwright unavailable.
code:
# This skill is executed via the /api/playwright/fetch endpoint
# Parameters:
#   url (string): Single URL to fetch
#   urls (array): Multiple URLs to fetch in parallel
#   timeout (number): Timeout in ms (default: 15000)
#   waitForJS (boolean): Wait for JavaScript to render (default: true)
#   includeLinks (boolean): Extract links from page (default: false)
#   maxLength (number): Max content length (default: 8000)
#
# Features:
# - Browser pooling for fast execution
# - Stealth mode with fingerprint randomization
# - Handles JavaScript-rendered content
# - Automatic bot detection avoidance
# - Falls back to axios if Playwright unavailable

name: playwright_interact
description: Interact with a webpage (click, type, scroll) before extracting content (for complex sites requiring user actions)
systemPrompt: Use this skill when you need to interact with a page before getting content (click buttons, fill forms, scroll to load content). Define a sequence of actions: click, type, wait, scroll, waitForNavigation. For simple page fetching, use playwright_fetch instead.
code:
# This skill is executed via the /api/playwright/interact endpoint
# Parameters:
#   url (string): URL to navigate to
#   actions (array): Actions to perform, each with:
#     - type: 'click', 'type', 'wait', 'scroll', 'waitForNavigation'
#     - selector: CSS selector for click/type/wait
#     - text: Text to type (for 'type' action)
#     - timeout: Max wait time (for 'wait' and 'waitForNavigation')
#   timeout (number): Overall timeout in ms (default: 30000)
#   maxLength (number): Max content length (default: 8000)
#
# Example actions:
#   [{"type": "click", "selector": "button.accept-cookies"}]
#   [{"type": "type", "selector": "input#search", "text": "query"}]
#   [{"type": "scroll"}]
#   [{"type": "waitForNavigation"}]

name: web_search
description: Search the web using DuckDuckGo with optional Playwright content fetching (returns actual page content, not just snippets)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to search the web for current information. Set fetchContent=true to get actual page content, not just snippets. Use timeRange for recent results: 'd' (day), 'w' (week), 'm' (month), 'y' (year). Queries with 'recent', 'latest', or 'news' are auto-enhanced with current date.
code:
# This skill is executed via the /api/search endpoint
# Parameters:
#   query (string): Search query
#   limit (number): Max number of results (default: 5)
#   fetchContent (boolean): Fetch actual page content (default: false)
#   contentLimit (number): Number of URLs to fetch content from (default: 3)
#   timeRange (string): d/w/m/y for day/week/month/year filter
#
# Features:
# - Auto-enhances 'recent/latest/news' queries with current date
# - Uses Playwright for content fetching when available
# - Falls back to axios for basic content extraction
# - Caches results for 1 hour
# - Returns extracted content including title, summary, headings, and paragraphs

name: create_pdf
description: Generate a PDF file from text content (tries reportlab, wkhtmltopdf, enscript; falls back to HTML if no PDF tools available)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to create PDF documents from text content. Tries multiple methods (reportlab, wkhtmltopdf, enscript) and falls back to HTML if none available. For complex layouts, create HTML first with create_file, then convert with html_to_pdf.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   outputPath (string): Path to save the PDF file
#   title (string): Document title (optional, default: 'Document')
#   content (string): Text content for the PDF
#
# PDF Generation Methods (tried in order):
# 1. Python reportlab library
# 2. wkhtmltopdf (converts HTML wrapper to PDF)
# 3. enscript + ps2pdf (plain text to PDF)
# 4. Fallback: Creates HTML file instead if no PDF tools available
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:create_pdf(outputPath="/tmp/report.pdf", title="My Report", content="Report content here")]

name: html_to_pdf
description: Convert an HTML file to PDF (tries wkhtmltopdf, weasyprint, or Chrome headless)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to convert HTML files to PDF. Tries wkhtmltopdf, WeasyPrint, or Chrome headless. Best for complex layouts with CSS styling. Create the HTML first with create_file if needed.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   htmlPath (string): Path to the HTML file to convert
#   outputPath (string): Path to save the PDF (optional, defaults to same name with .pdf extension)
#
# PDF Conversion Methods (tried in order):
# 1. wkhtmltopdf - Most common, fast conversion
# 2. WeasyPrint - Python-based, good CSS support
# 3. Chrome/Chromium headless - Print to PDF feature
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:html_to_pdf(htmlPath="/tmp/report.html", outputPath="/tmp/report.pdf")]

name: markdown_to_html
description: Convert a Markdown file to styled HTML (uses pandoc if available, otherwise built-in converter)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to convert Markdown to styled HTML. Uses pandoc if available, otherwise a built-in converter supporting headers, bold, italic, links, code blocks, and lists. Chain with html_to_pdf for Markdown to PDF conversion.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   mdPath (string): Path to the Markdown file to convert
#   outputPath (string): Path to save the HTML (optional, defaults to same name with .html extension)
#   title (string): Document title (optional, default: 'Document')
#
# Conversion Methods:
# 1. Pandoc - Full-featured Markdown processor
# 2. Built-in converter - Supports headers, bold, italic, links, code blocks, lists
#
# The built-in converter handles:
# - Headers (# ## ###)
# - Bold (**text**) and italic (*text*)
# - Links [text](url)
# - Code blocks (```code```)
# - Inline code (`code`)
# - Bullet lists (* item or - item)
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:markdown_to_html(mdPath="/tmp/notes.md", outputPath="/tmp/notes.html", title="My Notes")]

name: read_pdf
description: Extract text content from a PDF file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to extract text from PDF files. Tries pdftotext (poppler-utils) first, then PyPDF2. Returns extracted text from all pages. For page count or image extraction, use pdf_page_count or pdf_to_images.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   filePath (string): Path to the PDF file to read
#
# Text Extraction Methods (tried in order):
# 1. pdftotext (from poppler-utils)
# 2. Python PyPDF2 library
#
# Returns extracted text content from all pages.
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:read_pdf(filePath="/tmp/document.pdf")]

name: pdf_page_count
description: Get the number of pages in a PDF file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to quickly get the page count of a PDF without extracting content. Useful for large PDFs or when you just need the page count.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   filePath (string): Path to the PDF file
#
# Methods (tried in order):
# 1. pdfinfo (from poppler-utils)
# 2. Python PyPDF2 library
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:pdf_page_count(filePath="/tmp/document.pdf")]

name: pdf_to_images
description: Convert PDF pages to PNG images
systemPrompt: Use this skill to convert PDF pages to PNG images. Requires pdftoppm from poppler-utils. Creates one image per page (page-1.png, page-2.png, etc.). Useful for OCR processing or visual inspection.
code:
# This skill is executed client-side by Koda CLI
# Parameters:
#   filePath (string): Path to the PDF file
#   outputDir (string): Directory to save images (optional, defaults to current directory)
#
# Requires: pdftoppm from poppler-utils
#
# Output files are named: page-1.png, page-2.png, etc.
#
# Example:
# [SKILL:pdf_to_images(filePath="/tmp/document.pdf", outputDir="/tmp/pages")]

name: append_to_file
description: Append content to an existing file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to add content to the end of an existing file without overwriting it. Useful for log files, accumulating data, or adding new entries. Creates the file if it doesn't exist.

name: tail_file
description: Read the last N lines of a file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read the last lines of a file (like Unix tail). Default is 10 lines. Useful for log files, recent entries, or checking file endings without reading the whole file.

name: head_file
description: Read the first N lines of a file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read the first lines of a file (like Unix head). Default is 10 lines. Useful for previewing files, checking headers, or reading file beginnings without loading everything.

name: git_status
description: Get git repository status (modified, staged, untracked files)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to check the current state of a git repository: modified files, staged changes, untracked files, current branch. Defaults to current directory. Use before git_diff for a full picture.

name: git_diff
description: Get git diff of changes (staged or unstaged)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to see the actual changes in a git repository. Set staged=true for staged changes, false for working directory changes. Optionally specify a specific file path. Returns the diff output.

name: git_log
description: Get git commit history
systemPrompt: Use this skill to view commit history. Default limit is 10 commits. Set oneline=true for compact output. Useful for understanding recent changes, finding commit hashes, or reviewing project history.

name: git_branch
description: List git branches or get current branch
systemPrompt: Use this skill to list all branches in a git repository and identify the current branch. Useful for understanding branch structure or before switching branches.

name: get_env_var
description: Get environment variable value
systemPrompt: Use this skill to read environment variables. Useful for checking configuration, paths, or system settings. Returns the value or indicates if not set.

name: set_env_var
description: Set environment variable (current session only)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to set environment variables for the current session. Changes are NOT persistent across sessions. Useful for temporary configuration or testing.

name: which_command
description: Find the path of an executable command
systemPrompt: Use this skill to find where a command is installed (like Unix 'which'). Useful for verifying tool availability, debugging PATH issues, or finding the exact binary location.

name: disk_usage
description: Get disk usage information for a path
systemPrompt: Use this skill to check disk space: total, used, free, and percentage. Defaults to root filesystem. Useful for monitoring storage or before large file operations.

name: get_uptime
description: Get system uptime
systemPrompt: Use this skill to check how long the system has been running. Returns uptime in seconds and human-readable format. Useful for system monitoring or diagnostics.

name: list_ports
description: List open network ports
systemPrompt: Use this skill to see which network ports are in use and what processes are listening on them. Useful for debugging network issues, checking service availability, or finding port conflicts.

name: list_services
description: List system services and their status
systemPrompt: Use this skill to see running system services. Cross-platform: uses systemctl on Linux, launchctl on macOS, sc query on Windows. Useful for system administration and troubleshooting.

name: diff_files
description: Compare two files and show differences
systemPrompt: Use this skill to compare two files and see the differences. Returns a unified diff showing what changed between the files. Useful for code review, comparing versions, or verifying changes.

name: search_replace_file
description: Search and replace text in a file
systemPrompt: Use this skill to find and replace text in a file. Supports plain text or regex patterns. Returns the number of replacements made. For complex multi-line changes, use read_file and update_file instead.

name: tar_extract
description: Extract a tar archive (.tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tar.bz2)
systemPrompt: Use this skill to extract tar archives including compressed variants (.gz, .bz2, .xz). If extractPath is not specified, extracts to the same directory as the archive.

name: tar_create
description: Create a tar archive from files/directories
systemPrompt: Use this skill to create tar archives. Supports compression: 'gz' (gzip), 'bz2' (bzip2), 'xz', or '' (none). Directories are added recursively.

name: analyze_video
description: Analyze a video file for metadata, duration, resolution, frame rate, codec info, and optionally extract frames
systemPrompt: Use this skill to analyze video files. Returns metadata including duration, resolution, frame rate, codec, bit rate, and file size. Set extractFrames=true to extract sample frames at frameInterval seconds (default: 10). Frames are saved to outputDir (defaults to same directory as video). Supports common formats: MP4, AVI, MKV, MOV, WebM, etc.
